objective: Carmen completes evening med pass and care routines
opposition: Understaffing, resident needs, physical exhaustion of the work
turn: The routine itself reveals institutional dysfunction — shortcuts, thin coverage, the gap between care standards and care reality
consequence_cost: Carmen's body absorbs the cost; her exhaustion is not abstract but muscular and specific

objective: Carmen picks up Reggie from her mother's, manages domestic life
opposition: Financial pressure, time scarcity, the narrowness of her margins
turn: The domestic world mirrors the institutional one — precision under constraint, no slack in the system
consequence_cost: Carmen's life outside work has no more room than her life inside it

objective: Carmen administers Harry's evening medications
opposition: Harry's flash of cognitive clarity — he corrects her tramadol count
turn: Harry reveals sharp precision then immediately retreats into confusion; Carmen registers the crack but files it away
consequence_cost: A hairline fracture in Carmen's understanding of her patients; she sleeps on it rather than acting

objective: Carmen runs end-of-shift medication count and observes med pass carefully
opposition: Count discrepancies (3 oxycodone, 5 alprazolam short); Lola's theatrical pill-taking
turn: The discrepancies exceed charting-error range; Lola's performance is too practiced to be accidental
consequence_cost: Carmen documents the count under her name — her liability grows with each documented discrepancy

objective: Carmen works a double shift and walks the overnight halls
opposition: Animated coherent voices in the TV room at 1 AM that go slack when she appears
turn: The sundowning explanation fails — these residents were lucid, organized, and performing on cue
consequence_cost: Carmen can no longer explain the anomalies away; her attention is fully locked

objective: Harry runs the ring's midnight meeting: inventory, sorting, financial accounting
opposition: The clock (Devon might wake), operational logistics, awareness that Carmen is suspicious
turn: The meeting reveals the operation's precision and domestic absurdity simultaneously — pharmaceutical-grade competence mixed with arguments about coffee and compression socks
consequence_cost: Harry identifies Carmen as a genuine threat; the ring must decide how to handle her

objective: Ring members return to rooms and resume their performances
opposition: The transition from lucid selves back to performed impairment
turn: The ease of the switch is both comic and unsettling — these people toggle between identities with practiced fluency
consequence_cost: The performance is so habitual it no longer costs them visible effort, but Harry's awareness of Carmen adds new weight

objective: Carmen is charting overnight when she sees Harry walk unassisted at 2:40 AM
opposition: Harry walking with full motor control, carrying something in a towel
turn: Three seconds of mutual recognition — the pretense dissolves, then Harry reinstates it with 'Eleanor? Is that you?'
consequence_cost: Carmen now has visual proof she cannot rationalize; the confrontation is inevitable

objective: Carmen does a full manual medication count
opposition: The count itself — 47 pills short across four controlled substances
turn: The number transforms the discrepancy from documentation issue to diversion — this is not error, it's a system
consequence_cost: Carmen now holds evidence of a federal crime that occurred on her shifts, under her name

objective: Carmen confronts Harry in the TV room at midnight
opposition: Harry's initial attempt to maintain the facade, then his full-powered counter-arguments
turn: Harry drops the act completely — transformation of voice, posture, vocabulary — and reframes the situation: Carmen is trapped whether she reports or not
consequence_cost: Carmen gives Harry a week, revealing she's already past the point of simply reporting

objective: Carmen processes the confrontation alone
opposition: Her own conflicted position — the arguments she can't refute
turn: Carmen realizes Harry was right: she came to negotiate, not to report; the decision was already half-made
consequence_cost: Carmen's self-image as a rule-following professional takes a hit she can feel

objective: Marcus visits Lakeview as 'Lola's nephew' and picks up the package
opposition: The performance required — signing in, chatting with staff, maintaining cover
turn: The handoff is routine and invisible — pills in a cookie tin, hidden in plain sight during visiting hours
consequence_cost: The ease of the handoff reveals how little institutional oversight exists

objective: Marcus distributes to three regular buyers in a strip mall parking lot
opposition: Each buyer's medical and economic situation — they need these medications and can't afford them legitimately
turn: The buyers are not criminals or addicts but working people rationing healthcare; Marcus is a middleman in a broken system
consequence_cost: Marcus charges below street price; his $1,200/month margin barely covers rent

objective: Marcus calls Lola to confirm the count; she asks about his mother
opposition: Marcus's loneliness and his lie — his mother died two years ago
turn: Lola is the closest thing Marcus has to family; the phone call reveals his emotional dependence on the ring
consequence_cost: Marcus's connection to the residents is genuine, not transactional — which makes his later betrayal more painful

objective: Carmen picks up Reggie, shops, makes dinner, helps with homework
opposition: Financial precision, time pressure, the monotony of making a thin budget work
turn: The domestic routine is rendered with economic specificity — she hits the grocery budget to the dollar
consequence_cost: The ordinary evening reveals how narrow Carmen's margins are; there is no slack for a crisis

objective: Carmen calls Yolanda, then does the math of her life
opposition: Yolanda's story about the nurse who lost her license; the relentless arithmetic of Carmen's obligations
turn: The financial math crystallizes: $47K salary against loans, rent, car, insurance, childcare; one disruption collapses everything
consequence_cost: Carmen looks at Reggie sleeping and feels the choice not as ethics but as arithmetic

objective: Agnes's interior monologue: her career, the stroke, Helen's betrayal, life at Lakeview
opposition: Her rage at Helen, the institutional indifference, the waste of her pharmaceutical expertise
turn: Agnes stopped fighting and started performing — the surrender was strategic, not defeat
consequence_cost: Agnes's competence is wasted by a system that classified her based on a temporary speech deficit

objective: Agnes drops the act with Carmen during a routine check
opposition: Carmen's remaining assumption that the residents are vulnerable or impaired
turn: Agnes's precision dismantles Carmen's framework: 'Stop performing your own dilemma and come see what we actually do'
consequence_cost: Carmen must abandon her protective framing — these people don't need her advocacy, they need her complicity

objective: Cliff prepares the facility for the state survey
opposition: His own administrative panic; staff resistance to pointless documentation busywork
turn: The preparation reveals Cliff's priorities — flowers in the lobby, redone paperwork, no actual care improvements
consequence_cost: The institutional theater of compliance preparation mirrors the ring's theater of impairment

objective: The ring performs maximum impairment during surveyor interviews
opposition: The surveyors' questions, the risk of inconsistency, Burt's actual impairment
turn: Each performance is a comic set piece — Harry's elaborate wrong answers, Agnes's pharmaceutical ignorance act, Lola's time-wasting stories
consequence_cost: The performances succeed but Burt's genuine confusion next to the others' performed confusion creates a darkly comic contrast

objective: Carmen is interviewed about medication management and controlled substance tracking
opposition: The surveyor's specific questions about count discrepancies documented under Carmen's name
turn: Carmen lies fluently, blaming overnight staff and system issues — she commits a crime with professional competence
consequence_cost: Carmen sits in her car for fifteen minutes afterward, unable to drive; she has crossed a line she cannot uncross

objective: Harry shows Carmen the full operation: finances, distribution, buyer demographics
opposition: The scale of what Carmen is now protecting — 18 months, $48K total
turn: Agnes's records reveal a pharmaceutical arbitrage system, not a crime ring; the logic is economic and medical
consequence_cost: Carmen is now evaluating the operation's efficiency rather than its legality — her moral framework has shifted

objective: Carmen meets Rosa in the Lakeview parking lot
opposition: Rosa's medical and economic reality — lupus, no insurance, $400/month hydroxychloroquine
turn: Rosa's cost comparison makes the operation's logic unarguable on a human level
consequence_cost: Carmen can't argue with the math; the last barrier to full identification with the ring collapses

objective: Carmen responds to Burt's fall at 3 AM; realizes the pill stash is in his room
opposition: The EMTs arriving, the risk of the stash being found during room search or hospital belongings inventory
turn: Carmen takes the Hungarian-English dictionary and hides it in the supply closet — active evidence tampering
consequence_cost: Carmen has crossed from passive complicity to active criminal participation

objective: Burt returns from hospital more diminished
opposition: His progressive dementia — real, not performed — visible in his confusion and docility
turn: Burt's return shows he has noticeably declined; the fall accelerated what was already happening
consequence_cost: The ring confronts the reality that its members' bodies are failing regardless of their plans

objective: The ring meets to debate Burt's continued participation
opposition: Harry wants to keep using Burt's prescriptions; Agnes argues Burt can no longer consent
turn: Carmen says 'He'd want us to keep going' — startled by her own 'us'; Earl looks at Burt's empty wheelchair
consequence_cost: Carmen has stopped observing and started operating; the language of belonging slipped out before she could catch it

objective: Lola's interior life: the performance wearing on her, Tomás, her children
opposition: Her Parkinson's tremor, the fear that performance and decline are merging, the distance of her family
turn: Lola catches herself genuinely forgetting and can't tell if it's the act seeping in or actual age
consequence_cost: The psychological cost of sustained deception becomes concrete — Lola is losing the boundary between self and performance

objective: Lola and Tammy sit in the courtyard during Tammy's break
opposition: Lola's guilt about deceiving Tammy; Tammy's pain about her divorce
turn: Lola wants to say something real but can't without exposing everyone; she settles for affection that feels inadequate
consequence_cost: The courtyard scene — three scraggly trees, parking lot view — ends with honest affection that exists inside a fundamental dishonesty

objective: Marcus proposes the volume buyer to the ring
opposition: Agnes's opposition, Carmen's alarm, the fracture in the group's moral consensus
turn: Marcus frames it as pragmatism; the others see it as crossing their ethical line
consequence_cost: The proposal tests the ring's justification and finds it fragile — the 'helping people' logic doesn't survive a reseller

objective: Five-way argument: each member states their position on the volume buyer
opposition: Five distinct positions pulling the decision in different directions
turn: Earl's one-sentence verdict ('We started this because nobody was looking out for us') and Carmen's ultimatum create a fragile no; Marcus agrees too fast
consequence_cost: Marcus's quick agreement is the false note that sets up his later unilateral action

objective: Tammy's daily life: work, the divorce, the apartment, the paycheck
opposition: Burnout, loneliness, the indignity of $16.50/hour with no benefits
turn: The chapter reveals that Tammy's cynicism is protective, not constitutive — she cares more than she shows
consequence_cost: Tammy's competence and observation skills are dormant, not absent; she's been conserving energy, not losing it

objective: Tammy notices patterns: Carmen's behavior, Agnes's eyes, Harry's variable confusion
opposition: Carmen's obliviousness to what Tammy can see; the assumption that Tammy is too burned out to notice
turn: Tammy decides to watch rather than report, and stays late — she sees the TV room light at 12:30 AM
consequence_cost: A second person is converging on the truth through a completely independent investigation; Carmen doesn't know

objective: Earl takes the facility van to Maryann's grave, makes a drop to Marcus
opposition: The logistics of the outing, the emotional weight of the grave visit, the drop executed mid-trip
turn: Earl talks to Maryann about the grandkids, doesn't mention the ring; the drop happens through the van window
consequence_cost: Earl's dual purpose — genuine grief and operational obligation — coexist without either diminishing the other

objective: Earl alone with his cough
opposition: His refusal to seek diagnosis, the fear of what testing would reveal and what it would cost the ring
turn: Earl checks the handkerchief for blood; no blood today, but the check itself reveals how long he's been watching for it
consequence_cost: Earl's hidden illness is both sacrifice (protecting the ring's supply) and denial (refusing to face mortality)

objective: Agnes intercepts Marcus's accidental text; Harry discovers Marcus went rogue
opposition: The evidence of Marcus's unilateral deal — quantities exceeding anything the ring has moved
turn: Harry's fury is cold and precise; the accountant's anger, controlled but absolute
consequence_cost: The operation is compromised by Marcus's independent action; the ring has lost control of its own supply chain

objective: Ring confronts Marcus in a midnight meeting
opposition: Marcus's counter-argument: he takes the real risk, they'd get sympathy while he'd go to prison; his distribution leverage
turn: Marcus's argument has moral weight — he IS the most exposed — but his actions were a betrayal of collective trust
consequence_cost: Earl's 'You're not one of us anymore' hurts Marcus more than the operational consequences

objective: Aftermath: Harry tells Carmen the ring's origin story; they sit together
opposition: The weight of what's been lost — Marcus's departure, the operation's vulnerability
turn: Harry's origin story reframes everything: the ring started because the facility was failing to provide their medications safely
consequence_cost: Carmen and Harry sit together in the TV room — his age is real, the silence is heavy, the question is what now

objective: News breaks of the volume buyer's arrest; Marcus goes dark
opposition: The arrest on local news; Marcus's phone off, no visits; the exposure chain: buyer to Marcus to Lakeview
turn: The ring panics — if investigators connect the buyer to Marcus to the facility, everything unravels
consequence_cost: The external threat is not theoretical; it's a news broadcast with a suspect's face

objective: Carmen and the ring destroy all evidence overnight
opposition: The physical labor of disposal — small batches of pills, burning notebooks, breaking the phone
turn: Agnes watches her notebooks burn; Earl helps despite his cough; Lola's tremor worsens; Harry directs from his wheelchair but seems genuinely diminished
consequence_cost: The evidence is gone, but so is the operation — pills flushed, records burned, the communication destroyed

objective: Dawn: Carmen tells Harry it's over; Tammy arrives early; Harry makes a phone call
opposition: Carmen's finality versus Harry's resistance; Tammy's silent observation; Harry's private call to an unknown number
turn: Carmen: 'It wasn't good. It was necessary.' The distinction matters to her. Harry agrees, then makes a call she doesn't see.
consequence_cost: The operation appears to end, but Harry's phone call is the final unresolved note — he may not be done

objective: Carmen works a day shift; observes the changed residents
opposition: Each ring member's visible diminishment: Lola subdued, Agnes not eating, Earl submitting to testing, Harry fading
turn: The performances have changed — not because the deception has been discovered but because without the ring's purpose, the performers are losing their reason to perform
consequence_cost: Carmen has applied for a hospital job; she's leaving not from fear but from the inability to watch these people return to the lives they'd escaped

objective: Final conversation with Harry; Carmen drives home with Reggie
opposition: Harry's last revelation: the ring started as quality control when Agnes caught a dangerous drug interaction and was ignored
turn: Harry: 'We were taking care of ourselves because no one else was going to.' Marcus's voicemail: 'Found another way. Call me.'
consequence_cost: Carmen drives with the phone face-down and Reggie asleep — she doesn't delete, doesn't call; the road is open in both directions
